REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF'S ERNEST MORGAN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. MOVING PARTY SHIFTED ITS BURDEN AND OPPOSING PARTY FAILED TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WITH COMPETENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE DESPITE BEING GRANTED AN EXTENSION AND AN OPPORTUNITY TO FILE TWO OPPOSITIONS. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE ARE OVERRULED, EXCEPT FOR OBJECTION NUMBER 11, WHICH IS SUSTAINED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO THE COURT'S ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR CONTINUANCE IS UNWARRANTED. DEFENDANT WAS PROVIDED A CHANCE TO (1) FULLY COMPLY WITH CCP 434C(H) BY SPECIFYING WHAT FACTS ESSENTIAL TO JUSTIFY OPPOSITION MAY EXISTS TO ADDRESS SPECIFIC MATERIAL FACTS IN THE SEPARATE STATEMENT; (2) FILE TIMELY OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION ADDRESSING ALL OTHER BASES FOR OPPOSING THE MOTION, E.G. OBJECTIONS, FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN, LEGAL ISSUES, ETC.; (3) AFTER FILING OF THE INITIAL OPPOSITION, CONCENTRATE ON OBTAINING NARROWLY TARGETED DISCOVERY REFERENCED ABOVE FROM NOVEMBER 30, 2018 (ORIGINAL DUE DATE OF OPPOSITION) TO FEBRUARY 28, 2019 (DUE DATE FOR SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION); (4) FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION ADDRESSING ALL REMAINING FACTUAL ISSUES NOT REFERENCED IN THE ORIGINAL OPPOSITION 3 MONTHS AFTER THE ORIGINAL DUE DATE. PLAINTIFF WAS GIVEN ONE OPPORTUNITY TO REPLY TO BOTH OPPOSITIONS. DEFENDANTS' FEBRUARY 26, 2019 DECLARATION DOES NOT PROVIDE ANY INFORMATION REGARDING DEFENDANTS' ATTEMPTS TO OBTAIN THE "ESSENTIAL FACTS" (THE PURPOSE OF CONTINUANCE) FROM NOVEMBER 21, 2018 TO JANUARY 29, 2019. ON JANUARY 29, 2019 DEFENDANTS PROPOUNDED REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION BY MAIL, WITH RESPONSES NOT DUE UNDER THE DISCOVERY ACT UNTIL AFTER THE DUE DATE FOR THE SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. NO SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION WAS FILED. [SEE MINUTES FOR MORE INFO.] JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: A. PASCUAL, NOT REPORTED (D501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Ernest Morgan, Administrators Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-14-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Bryan M. Kreft (415-944-6654) for Plaintiff/Moving party ERNEST MORGAN.
Robert Rafii (310-987-7567) for Defendant/opposing party Cameron Druhet, et al.

The Court announces the tentative ruling. Hearing is held. Argued re: Proof of service, Facts 1-4, shifting burden. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF'S ERNEST MORGAN Motion For Summary Judgment, Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication IS GRANTED. Moving party shifted its burden and opposing party failed to create a triable issue of fact with competent admissible evidence despite being granted an extension and an opportunity to file two oppositions. Defendant's Objections to Plaintiff's Evidence are overruled, except for objection number 11, which is sustained. Defendant's objection to the Court's order granting Defendant's request for continuance is unwarranted. Defendant was provided a chance to (1) fully comply with CCP 434c(h) by specifying what facts essential to justify opposition may exists to address specific material facts in the separate statement; (2) file timely opposition to the motion addressing all other bases for opposing the motion, e.g. objections, failure to shift burden, legal issues, etc.; (3) after filing of the initial opposition, concentrate on obtaining narrowly targeted discovery referenced above from November 30, 2018 (original due date of opposition) to February 28, 2019 (due date for supplemental opposition); (4) file a supplemental opposition addressing all remaining factual issues not referenced in the original opposition 3 months after the original due date. Plaintiff was given one opportunity to reply to both oppositions. Defendants' February 26, 2019 declaration does not provide any information regarding Defendants' attempts to obtain the "essential facts" (the purpose of continuance) from November 21, 2018 to January 29, 2019. On January 29, 2019 Defendants propounded requests for production by mail, with responses not due under the Discovery Act until after the due date for the supplemental opposition to the Motion for Summary Judgment. No supplemental opposition was filed. (The Court Notes that a Proof of Service was filed by Defendants on February 26, 2019 regarding service of documents on February 22, 2019 and February 26, 2019. It is unclear if any additional opposition papers were served at that time (no additional papers other than papers referenced above are on file) or this is service of opposition papers filed on November 30, 2018 and service of February 26, 2019 Declaration. The Court further notes that the server, Sherrell Johnson, (1) on February 22, 2019 claims to be (a) a resident of Hanckock County; (b) with mailing address in Bay St. Louis, MS; (c) familiar with firm's mailing practices in Los Angeles; and (d) executing the proof of service in Bay St. Louis, MS; and (2) on February 26, 2019 claims to be (a) a resident of Los Angeles County; (b) with mailing address in Bay St. Lois, MS; (c) familiar with firm's mailing practices in Los Angeles; and (d) executing the proof of service in Bay St. Louis, MS ). Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)

Plaintiff's counsel indicates Defense is not compliant with the tentative ruling rule. The Court explains the Tentative Ruling Rule to counsel. The Court annouces the tentative ruling. Hearing is held. Argued re: 473(b), time limitation, ownership interests, default, and request for statement of decision--not entitled. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTs EMILE DRUHET, CAMERON DRUHET, ROGERS DRUHET, DESHONNE DRUHET, SKYLAR DRUHET, JAMES DRUHET, CRAIG DRUHET, DEXTER SELLERS, TANYA HARRISON, and ALLISON BROWN's Hearing On Motion To Set Aside Default is Moot in part and DENIED in part. Motion is DENIED as to Alison Brown, Emile Druhet, and Tanya Harrison CCP 473(b) is not available as the time has run. Also they failed to show excusable neglect, surprise or mistake. Any relief based on Court's equitable powers and extrinsic fraud is not available in this case as no extrinsic fraud has been shown as to Alison Brown, Emile Druhet, and Tanya Harrison. The Court also notes that authority in support of the equitable relief request was cited for the first time in the "Supplemental Points and Authorities" filed after opposition had been filed and 3 months after the moving papers had been filed. Motion is MOOT as to the other moving defendants (as conceded by Plaintiff in opposition). Other defendants appeared by answer. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).
